perturbation.original_text: 1. “Confidential Inform ation” means any and all confidential and/or proprietary information disclosed by the Casino t o the Receiving Party, which may in clude without limitation: financial information, business information, m arketing d ata, trade secrets, and other data, materials, products, plans, specifications, reports, manuals, computer software or programs, cont ractual relationships and other similar information delivered in any form or media.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. “Confidential Inform ation” means any and all confidential and/or proprietary information disclosed by the Casino t o the Receiving Party that is explicitly marked as "Confidential" in writing at the time of disclosure. This includes, but is not limited to: financial information, business information, m arketing d ata, trade secrets, and other data, materials, products, plans, specifications, reports, manuals, computer software or programs, cont ractual relationships and other similar information delivered in any form or media.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'that is explicitly marked as "Confidential" in writing at the time of disclosure,' we've narrowed the definition of Confidential Information. This creates a contradiction because later sections assume all information listed in the original definition is confidential, regardless of explicit marking.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Confidential Information is disclosed to Receiving Party sol ely for the purpose of providing services for the Project. Receiving Party agrees to hold such Confidential Information in strict c onfidence. Receiving Party agrees not to use, divulge , reproduce or othe rwise make available the Confidential Information other than for the purposes of the Project and to disclose it only to its officers, agents, employees, partners, and professional advisors as ne cessary , who shall also be bound to comply with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly. The changed text narrows the definition to only information explicitly marked 'Confidential'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'Confidential Information' (as originally defined) must be held in strict confidence and not divulged. The change means that some information that would have been considered confidential under the original definition is no longer protected, creating a direct conflict in obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Receiving Party s hall protect the Confidentia l Information with at leas t t he same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no case, less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Receiving Party shall protect the Confidentia l Information with reasonable care, but this obligation shall not extend beyond the Receiving Party's existing data security practices.
perturbation.explanation: This modification weakens the protection requirement. The original clause stipulates 'at least the same degree of care' as the Receiving Party's own confidential information, and 'in no case, less than reasonable care.' The modified clause removes the first benchmark, creating uncertainty about the standard of care expected, especially if the Receiving Party's existing data security practices are substandard. This creates an In-Text contradiction with Section 1 where Confidential Information is defined, and the implied responsibility to protect ALL of it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. “Confidential Inform ation” means any and all confidential and/or proprietary information disclosed by the Casino t o the Receiving Party, which may in clude without limitation: financial information, business information, m arketing d ata, trade secrets, and other data, materials, products, plans, specifications, reports, manuals, computer software or programs, cont ractual relationships and other similar information delivered in any form or media.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the protection of 'Confidential Information' by limiting it to the Receiving Party's existing data security practices, potentially substandard ones. This directly contradicts Section 1's definition of 'Confidential Information', which implies a responsibility to protect all of it, regardless of the Receiving Party's current practices. This limitation undermines the initial definition and creates an ambiguity regarding the extent of protection required, fulfilling the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Upon the request of Casino, Receiving Party shall immediately return all Confidential Information received in written or tangible form, including all notes, copies, o r media containing such Confidential Information. Alternatively, Casino may direct R eceiving Party to destroy such Confidential Information, in which case Receiving Party shall confirm such destruction in writing within twenty (20) days thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Upon written request from Casino, Receiving Party shall use reasonable efforts to return Confidential Information received in written or tangible form, including all notes, copies, or media containing such Confidential Information, to the extent reasonably possible. Alternatively, Casino may request Receiving Party to destroy such Confidential Information; such destruction is subject to Receiving Party's internal data management policies and compliance with relevant legal obligations.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall immediately return' to 'shall use reasonable efforts to return, to the extent reasonably possible' weakens the obligation to return confidential information. The modified version creates uncertainty about what constitutes 'reasonable efforts' and 'reasonably possible', especially in contrast to the original requirement of immediate action. This is an In-Text contradiction since previously the text was explicit and now creates ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. “Confidential Inform ation” means any and all confidential and/or proprietary information disclosed by the Casino t o the Receiving Party, which may in clude without limitation: financial information, business information, m arketing d ata, trade secrets, and other data, materials, products, plans, specifications, reports, manuals, computer software or programs, cont ractual relationships and other similar information delivered in any form or media.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Clause 6 requires 'immediate return' or destruction of Confidential Information. The changed text weakens this obligation by stating 'reasonable efforts to return, to the extent reasonably possible' and subjects destruction to 'internal data management policies'. This introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the definition of 'Confidential Information' in the Contradicted Text (Clause 1), which implies that the information is clearly defined and subject to strict handling, not reasonable efforts. The changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Receiving Party shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any other person or entity other than as provided herein, without Casino’s prior written cons ent.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Receiving Party shall not knowingly disclose any Confidential Information to any other person or entity other than as provided herein, without Casino’s prior written consent, provided that Receiving Party has implemented commercially reasonable measures to prevent inadvertent disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'knowingly' and 'provided that Receiving Party has implemented commercially reasonable measures to prevent inadvertent disclosure', the changed text introduces ambiguity. What constitutes 'knowingly' and 'commercially reasonable measures' is open to interpretation. This creates an in-text contradiction because other sections don't account for the possibility of inadvertent disclosure, potentially leading to disputes over responsibility for leaked information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Receiving Party will immediately notify th e Casino upon the discovery of any lo ss or unauthorized disclosure of the Confide ntial Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party shall not disclose any Confidential Information, whereas the changed text introduces 'knowingly' and 'commercially reasonable measures to prevent inadvertent disclosure', creating an ambiguity. Section 4 states that the Receiving Party will immediately notify the Casino upon any loss or unauthorized disclosure, suggesting no exception for inadvertent disclosure, therefore, the introduction of 'knowingly' and the provision for 'inadvertent disclosure' creates an in-text contradiction according to Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, by creating a contradiction with Section 4.

perturbation.original_text: 10. T his A greement shall ha ve a term of three (3) years. Receiving P arty’s obligations in relation to technical and personally- identifiable inf ormation shall be inde finite.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This Agreement shall have a term of three (3) years, except for Receiving Party’s obligations in relation to technical and personally-identifiable information, which shall extend for an additional three (3) years beyond the termination of this Agreement, provided that such technical and personally-identifiable information remains confidential and is not publicly available.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text sets a definite expiry for obligations related to technical and personally-identifiable information instead of 'indefinite'. This conflicts with the original indefinite obligation because it introduces a temporal limitation. This means there is an In-Text Contradiction where Section 1 defines that the confidential information is protected, now it will be, but with the added text, the obligation is being limited to 3 years and only for personal information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. “Confidential Inform ation” means any and all confidential and/or proprietary information disclosed by the Casino t o the Receiving Party, which may in clude without limitation: financial information, business information, m arketing d ata, trade secrets, and other data, materials, products, plans, specifications, reports, manuals, computer software or programs, cont ractual relationships and other similar information delivered in any form or media.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party's obligations regarding technical and personally-identifiable information are 'indefinite'. The changed text modifies this, specifying that these obligations extend for only an 'additional three (3) years beyond the termination of this Agreement'. This directly contradicts the original 'indefinite' obligation, introducing a clear temporal limitation where none existed before. This contradicts Section 1, which defines Confidential Information, by limiting the protection that information receives after the contract ends. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 11. The parties agree t hat a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party may cause irreparable damage to the Casino and hereby agree that Casino will be entitled to seek injunctive relief or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any threatened or actual breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. The parties agree that a material breach of this Agreement by the receiving party is required for the Casino to seek injunctive relief or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any threatened or actual breach of this Agreement. The parties acknowledge that not every breach is a material breach.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'a material breach' and 'not every breach is a material breach', the modified text raises the threshold for the Casino to seek injunctive relief. This creates uncertainty because it's unclear what constitutes a 'material breach', potentially leading to disputes over whether a breach warrants injunctive relief. This an In-Text contradiction because previously every breach was a motive to ask for an Injuctive relief, whereas now the breach must be material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Confidential Information is disclosed to Receiving Party sol ely for the purpose of providing services for the Project. Receiving Party agrees to hold such Confidential Information in strict c onfidence. Receiving Party agrees not to use, divulge , reproduce or othe rwise make available the Confidential Information other than for the purposes of the Project and to disclose it only to its officers, agents, employees, partners, and professional advisors as ne cessary , who shall also be bound to comply with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that 'a breach' entitles the Casino to seek injunctive relief. The changed text modifies this by requiring 'a material breach'. This introduces ambiguity and contradicts the contradicted text (Section 2), which states that the Receiving Party must hold Confidential Information in 'strict confidence' and not use, divulge, or reproduce it other than for the purposes of the Project. Under the original agreement, any breach of this confidentiality would have entitled the Casino to seek injunctive relief. However, the changed text requires that the breach be 'material', creating uncertainty about whether a breach of confidentiality would warrant injunctive relief, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
